data.title: SunPower expands equity line of credit to $55M
data.description:
• SunPower (SPWR) entered into an amendment to increase its equity line of credit with White Lion Capital from $30M to $55M.
  • The expanded ELOC provides SunPower with additional flexible access to capital at its discretion.
  • Under the ELOC structure, White Lion Capital agrees to purchase newly issued shares.
  • The facility allows SunPower to manage liquidity needs while minimizing potential shareholder dilution compared to a traditional equity offering.

data.title: U.S. Bancorp to acquire BTIG for up to $1B
data.description:
U.S. Bancorp (USB) on Tuesday announced a definitive agreement to acquire the financial services firm BTIG for up to $1B.

The purchase price is targeted to be $725M, comprising $362.5M of cash and 6,600,594 shares of common stock. The deal also includes up to an additional $275M of cash consideration payable over three years.

USB shares were -0.15% pre-market to $54.31.

The transaction is expected to have negligible 2026 EPS impact and decrease U.S. Bancorp's common equity Tier 1 capital ratio by ~12 basis points.

The two companies have worked closely over the past decade.

The transaction is expected to close in the second quarter.

BTIG leadership team will continue to lead the business after joining U.S. Bancorp. BTIG CEO LeRoy will report to Stephen Philipson, vice chair and head of wealth, corporate, commercial, and institutional banking at U.S. Bancorp.

data.title: TryHard Holdings authorizes up to $10M share repurchase program
data.description:
• TryHard Holdings’ (THH) approved a share repurchase program allowing the company to buy back up to $10M of its shares.
  • The program is effective immediately and will run through December 31, 2028, unless ended earlier by the board.

data.title: Cardinal Health raises FY26 outlook above consensus
data.description:
Cardinal Health (CAH) traded higher in the premarket on Tuesday after the pharmaceutical distributor increased its adjusted earnings outlook for fiscal 2026 to a level above the consensus ahead of its presentation at the J.P. Morgan Healthcare Conference.

The company stated that due to outperformance in its five operating segments, its FY26 non-GAAP diluted EPS is now expected to reach at least $10.00, compared to $9.65 - $9.85 in the prior guidance and $9.83 projected by analysts.

“This increased outlook is driven by strong performance and execution across the company's five operating segments,” the Dublin, Ohio-based firm said as its stock added ~3% in reaction, while its main rivals McKesson (MCK) and Cencora (COR) moved marginally higher.

Additionally, strong demand for specialty distribution, expansion of its MSO platforms, and more than 30% revenue growth projected for BioPharma Solutions have led to a revised projection for the company’s specialty revenues, which now stands at $50B, indicating a three-year CAGR of 16%.

Cardinal Health (CAH) is scheduled to present at the J.P. Morgan Healthcare Conference at 12:00 p.m. ET.

data.title: Maple Leaf Foods issues 2026 guidance, raises dividend 10%
data.description:
• Maple Leaf Foods (MFI:CA) announced it expects 2026 mid-single-digit revenue growth in 2026, driven by brand investment, innovation, U.S. expansion, and strong consumer demand for protein.
  • Adjusted EBITDA is projected at ~$520M–540M, supported by revenue growth, margin improvement, and the Fuel for Growth initiative.
  • Capital expenditures are expected to be ~$160M–180M, focused on maintenance, productivity, and automation.
  • The board approved an ~10% increase in the quarterly dividend to $0.21 per share, marking the company’s 11th consecutive annual dividend increase.

data.title: Amerigo expects to produce 63.8 M lbs of copper and 1.5 M lbs of molybdenum in 2026
data.description:
• Amerigo Resources (ARG:CA) on Tuesday said in 2026, it expects to produce 63.8 M lbs of copper and 1.5 M lbs of molybdenum.
  • Under the Assumptions, Amerigo’s 2026 cash cost is expected to be $1.98/lb. 
  • Projected 2026 EBITDA under the assumptions is expected to be $74.5 million.
  • The projected Free Cash Flow to be generated in 2026, under the Assumptions and Capex, is expected to be $34.8 million. It is projected that each $0.20/lb increase in the copper price could increase FCF by $4.1 million on average. 
  • In 2025, Minera Valle Central, the company’s 100% owned operation located near Rancagua, Chile, produced 62.2 million pounds of copper (2024: 64.6 M lbs), exceeding the company’s revised guidance of 60 to 61.5 M lbs of copper issued on October 8, 2025. In 2025, MVC delivered 62.6 M lbs of copper (2024: 65.0 M Lbs).
  • Molybdenum production and sales were 1.5 M lbs (2024: 1.3 M lbs), significantly exceeding guidance of 1.3 M lbs.
  • In Q4-2025, MVC produced 18.9 M lbs of copper and 0.5 M lbs of molybdenum at a cash cost of $1.93/lb and a normalized cash cost of $1.72/lb.
  • Amerigo’s 2025 average annual copper price was $4.73 per pound (“/lb”) (2024: $4.15/lb), and the average provisional copper price in Q4-2025 was $5.35/lb, compared to $4.54/lb in Q3-2025.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Cineplex reports December box office revenue of $67.0 mln
data.description:
• Cineplex (CGX:CA) on Tuesday said its December 2025 box office revenue rose to $67.0 million, up from $64.8 million a year earlier, according to a monthly results release.

  • The cinema operator reported fourth-quarter box office revenue of $140.7 million, down from $147.7 million for the same period in 2024. October and November box office revenues declined year-on-year, while December exceeded the prior year’s level.

  • Cineplex said December results were driven by franchise releases including Avatar: Fire and Ash, with about 90% of the film’s box office generated from premium formats.
  • Family, animated and international titles also contributed, with Hindi-language film Dhurandhar delivering a record performance in North America and accounting for more than 30% of the film’s regional box office at Cineplex locations.

  • The company also cited strong demand for alternative programming, including sold-out screenings of the Stranger Things series finale, and said its distribution arm Cineplex Pictures performed well in December.

  • CGX:CA closed +0.09% at $11.1.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Biggest stock movers Tuesday: LHX, CAH, DAL and more
data.description:
Stock index futures were little changed on Tuesday as investors waited for the latest inflation data.

JPMorgan Chase (JPM) issued fresh guidance for 2026 on Tuesday, with its view for overall net interest income better than consensus and its expectation for expenses in line.

Here are some of Tuesday's biggest stock movers:

 

Biggest stock gainers

  • L3Harris Technologies (LHX) +14% Shares rose after the defense contractor  announced a proposed $1B investment from the U.S. Department of Defense to significantly increase capacity to build solid rocket motors that power vital U.S. and allied missiles.
  • Cardinal Health (CAH) +3.4% - The company increased its expectations for fiscal year 2026 non-GAAP diluted earnings per share to at least $10.00, from its prior guidance range of $9.65 to $9.85. It expects that its specialty revenues will surpass $50 billion in fiscal 2026, marking a 16% compounded annual growth rate (CAGR) over three years.
  • Revvity (RVTY) +8.6% - Shares gained as the company now  expects its full-year adjusted earnings per share to exceed the upper-end of the $4.90-$5.00 guidance provided on October 27, 2025. Reported and organic revenue growth for the fourth quarter is expected to be approximately 6% and 4%, respectively, as compared to the same period a year ago. 

Biggest stock losers

  • Concentrix (CNXC) 4.9% - Shares fell after the technology company forecast 2026 earnings per share that missed the average analyst estimate. First quarter reported revenue was projected at $2.475 billion to $2.500 billion vs $2.49B consensus. 
  • Delta Air Lines (DAL) 5.1% - Stock slipped in premarket trading after reporting Q4 results and setting profit guidance. The airline company reported operating revenue of $16.0B, operating income of $1.5B with an operating margin of 9.2%, pre-tax income of $1.5B with a pre-tax margin of 9.5%, and earnings per share of $1.55 for the quarter vs. $1.55 consensus and $1.55 a year ago.
  • Travere Therapeutics (TVTX) 28.7% - Shares dropped after the company said it expects U.S. preliminary net product sales for Q4 2025 to be about $127M. The company expects FY25 U.S. net product sales of about $410M, and the year ended with roughly $323M in cash and marketable securities.

data.title: Global Atomic announces bought deal public offering for gross proceeds of C$25M
data.description:
• Global Atomic (GLATF) on Tuesday said it has entered into an agreement with Red Cloud Securities, as lead underwriter and bookrunner, pursuant to which Red Cloud has agreed to purchase for resale 28.4M units of the company at a price of C$0.88 per unit on a "bought deal" basis for gross proceeds of approximately C$25M.
  • Each Unit will consist of one common share of the company and one common share purchase warrant. Each warrant will entitle the holder thereof to purchase one common share at a price of C$1.15 at any time on or before that date which is 36 months following the closing date.
  • The company intends to use to use the net proceeds for the advancement of the company's Dasa Project and for general working capital purposes.
  • The closing of the brokered offering is expected to occur on or around January 29, 2026. 
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Bravo Mining announces C$50M bought deal financing
data.description:
• Bravo Mining (BRVO:CA) entered into a bought deal agreement with underwriters led by BMO Capital Markets and National Bank Capital Markets to raise ~C$50M through the sale of 11.37M shares at C$4.40/share.
  • The underwriters have a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional 15% of the offering to cover over-allotments.
  • The offering is expected to close on or about January 20, 2026.
  • Bravo also signed a non-binding term sheet with Orion Mine Finance, which plans to invest C$34.75M in a concurrent private placement at the same price.
  • Orion is expected to receive participation rights in future equity financings and matching rights on project or acquisition financing.
  • Subject to milestones, Orion has indicated potential financing support of up to US$300M through equity, debt, or other instruments.
  • Proceeds will be used to advance the Luanga PGM+Au+Ni Project toward feasibility studies, expand mineral resources, continue exploration, and fund general working capital.
